title: Small cell thermal control
abstract: A method (500) of controlling a temperature of a femtocell (200) includes receiving, at data processing hardware (210) of the femtocell, temperature measurements (232) from a temperature sensor (230) configured to measure a temperature of at least one of the data processing hardware or a power amplifier (220) of the femtocell. The method further includes determining, by the data processing hardware, whether the femtocell is operating above a threshold temperature based on the temperature measurements. When the femtocell is operating above the threshold temperature, the method includes modifying, by the data processing hardware, a power consumption characteristic of the femtocell that results in a power consumption reduction of at least one of the data processing hardware or the power amplifier.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A method (500) comprising: receiving, at data processing hardware (210) of a femtocell (200), temperature measurements (232) from a temperature sensor (230) configured to measure a temperature of at least one of the data processing hardware (210) or a power amplifier (220) of the femtocell (200); determining, by the data processing hardware (210), whether the femtocell (200) is operating above a threshold temperature based on the temperature measurements (232); and when the femtocell (200) is operating above the threshold temperature, modifying, by the data processing hardware (210), a power consumption characteristic of the femtocell (200) that results in a power consumption reduction of at least one of the data processing hardware (210) or the power amplifier (220). 2. The method (500) of claim 1 , wherein modifying the power consumption characteristic comprises reducing a physical downlink shared channel frequency resource and/or a physical uplink shared channel frequency resource to communicate with user equipment (130). 3. The method (500) of claim 2, wherein reducing the physical downlink shared channel frequency resource comprises scheduling a fewer number of user equipment (130) per downlink subframe (410, 410d) of a corresponding downlink communication frame (400), and reducing the physical uplink shared channel frequency resource to communicate with user equipment (130) comprises scheduling a fewer number of user equipments (130) per uplink subframe (410, 410u) of a corresponding uplink communication frame (400). 4. The method (500) of claim 2, wherein reducing the physical downlink shared channel frequency resource and/or the physical uplink shared channel frequency resource comprises scheduling a fewer number of resource blocks (440) per user equipment (130). 5. The method (500) of any of claims 1â4, wherein modifying the power consumption characteristic comprises reducing a power spectral density of the power amplifier (220). 5 6. The method (500) of claim 5, wherein the power spectral density is a measure of a physical downlink shared channel. 7. The method (500) of any of claims 1-6, wherein modifying the power consumption characteristic comprises reducing a number of user equipment (130) o communicating with the femtocell (200). 8. The method (500) of any of claims 1-7, wherein modifying the power consumption characteristic comprises changing a reception mode of one or more user equipment (130) communicating with the femtocell (200) to a discontinuous mode.5 9. The method (500) of any of claims 1-8, wherein modifying the power consumption characteristic comprises reducing a transmission duty cycle of the femtocell (200). 0 10. The method (500) of any of claims 1-9, wherein modifying the power consumption characteristic comprises reducing a reception duty cycle of the femtocell (200). 11. A femtocell (200) comprising: 5 a power amplifier (220); data processing hardware (210); a temperature sensor (230) configured to measure a temperature of at least one of the data processing hardware (210) or the power amplifier (220); and memory hardware (212) in communication with the data processing hardware0 (210), the memory hardware (212) storing instructions that when executed on the data 

 processing hardware (210) cause the data processing hardware (210) to perform operations comprising: receiving temperature measurements (232) from the temperature sensor (230); determining whether the femtocell (200) is operating above a threshold temperature based on the temperature measurements (232); and when the femtocell (200) is operating above the threshold temperature, modifying a power consumption characteristic that results in a power consumption reduction of at least one of the data processing hardware (210) or the power amplifier (220). 12. The femtocell (200) of claim 11, wherein modifying the power consumption characteristic comprises reducing a physical downlink shared channel frequency resource and/or a physical uplink shared channel frequency resource to communicate with user equipment (130). 13. The femtocell (200) of claim 12, wherein reducing the physical downlink shared channel frequency resource comprises scheduling a fewer number of user equipment (130) per downlink subframe (410, 410d) of a corresponding downlink communication frame (400), and reducing the physical uplink shared channel frequency resource to communicate with user equipment (130) comprises scheduling a fewer number of user equipments (130) per uplink subframe (410, 410u) of a corresponding uplink communication frame (400). 14. The femtocell (200) of claim 12, wherein reducing the physical downlink shared channel frequency resource and/or the physical uplink shared channel frequency resource comprises scheduling a fewer number of resource blocks (440) per user equipment (130). 15. The femtocell (200) of any of claims 11-14, wherein modifying the power consumption characteristic comprises reducing a power spectral density of the power amplifier (220). 16. The femtocell (200) of claim 15, wherein the power spectral density is a measure of a physical downlink shared channel. 17. The femtocell (200) of any of claims 11-16, wherein modifying the power consumption characteristic comprises reducing a number of user equipment (130) communicating with the femtocell (200). 18. The femtocell (200) of any of claims 11-17, wherein modifying the power consumption characteristic comprises changing a reception mode of one or more user equipment (130) communicating with the femtocell (200) to a discontinuous mode. 19. The femtocell (200) of any of claims 11-18, wherein modifying the power consumption characteristic comprises reducing a transmission duty cycle of the femtocell (200). 20. The femtocell (200) of any of claims 11-19, wherein modifying the power consumption characteristic comprises reducing a reception duty cycle of the femtocell (200).
